calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-28-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Continued To Mar-18-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer E. Choi, Deputy City Attorney, Office of the SF City Attorney, 415.554.3887, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Steven S. Miyake, Esq. of the 415.421.4400, appearing as the moving party/counsel for Defendants Villa Jacinto and Veronica Casilla.

THE LAW OFFICE OF STEVEN S. MIYAKE'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (AS TO VERONICA CASILLA).

Matter called, no argument is held. The parties stated that they are in agreement with a trial date continuance. The Court GRANTED the motion and inquired whether or not Mr. Miyake's proposed order contained all contact information for Mr. Miyake's now former client's and listed all future dates. Mr. Miyake stated the that the order did contain the required information. Discussions with regard to the trial date continuance were held. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court passed the matter for Mr. Miyake to contact his clients regarding a new trial date.

At the second call of the matter, Mr. Miyake stated that his clients were in agreement with a new trial date of March 19, 2018. The Court continued the November 5, 2018 trial date to March 18, 2019 per the agreement of the parties. All discovery dates are triggerd by the new trial date. An order granting the motion to be relieved as counsel was signed as to Defendant Veronica Casilla. In addition, the Court signed an order continuing the trial date. (see signed orders for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

THE LAW OFFICE OF STEVEN S. MIYAKE'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (AS TO VILLA JACINTO).

Matter called, no argument is held. The parties stated that they are in agreement with a trial date continuance. Mr. Miyake stated the that the order did contain the required information. Discussions with regard to the trial date continuance were held. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court passed the matter for Mr. Miyake to contact his clients regarding a new trial date.

At the second call of the matter, Mr. Miyake stated that his clients were in agreement with a new trial date of March 19, 2018.
